name: MUKHYAMANTRI NAUNIHAAL CHHAATRAVRTTI YOJANA, CHHATTISGARH
reward: A one-time annual scholarship of up to 10,000
eligibility: Wards of construction workers in Chhattisgarh studying in Class 1 till
about: Mukhyamantri Naunihaal Chhaatravrtti Yojana (MNCY), an initiative of the Government of Chhattisgarh, provides financial assistance to the children of construction workers registered with the Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers' Welfare Board, helping them pursue their education from Class 1 to postgraduate/Ph.D. level. Launched in 2017, the scheme aims to reduce the dropout rate and improve the educational attainment of these children
Eligible: Applicants must be dependent children of registered construction workers with the Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board.
The applicant must be enrolled in Class 1 to postgraduate/Ph.D. degree in an accredited school/college in Chhattisgarh.
Applicants must have an annual family income of up to ₹2,00,000.
note: There is no minimum age or academic requirement for the applicant. However, the applicant's parent or guardian must have worked as a registered construction worker in Chhattisgarh for at least 90 days within the preceding year.

After being admitted to a college, polytechnic, or other institution for mechanical or medical education, students receiving scholarships under this scheme must remain enrolled in their chosen program for a minimum of one year, covering at least one academic session.
If a student faces suspension during this period, they will be required to return the scholarship amount.

According to the scheme, the Assistant Labor Commissioner, Labor Officer, Assistant Labor Officer, Labor Inspector, and Labor Sub Inspector from all state labor offices are eligible to process scholarship applications.
After the scholarship is granted, the determined amount under the scheme will be transferred directly to the beneficiary's or their children's bank account via RTGS/NEFT/DBT.
In case of any discrepancy regarding the implementation of this scheme, the decision of the Secretary, Chhattisgarh Building and Other Construction Workers Welfare Board will be final and binding.
benefits: A one-time annual scholarship of up to 10,000
documents: Certificate issued by the Principal in the prescribed format (Note: The issued form should include a simple serial number and date, along with confirmation of admission for the current academic year.)
A scanned copy of the bank passbook
Copy of labor registration identity card
A scanned copy of the original Aadhaar card of the worker's son/daughter
Marksheet of previous qualifying examination
Employer certificate (if applicable)
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required details to Register. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID.)
Step 3: Navigate to the 'मुख्यमंत्री नौनिहाल छात्रवृत्ति योजना' and click on the given link.
Step 4: Fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents, and submit.

name: VIDYADHAN KARNATAKA CLASS 11 AND 12 SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM 2024
reward: A maximum of 210,000 per annum
eligibility: Class 11 and 12 students in a recognised school/institute of the state of Karnataka
about: Vidyadhan Karnataka Class 11 and 12 Scholarship Program 2024 is an opportunity offered by the Sarojini Damodaran Foundation for Class 10 passed students who are currently studying in Class 11 or 12 at a recognised school/institution of the state. The selected students will receive a maximum of ₹10,000 per annum. Sarojini Damodaran Foundation was established in 1999 by SD Shibulal (Co-founder and former CEO, Infosys) and Kumari Shibulal, (Sarojini Damodaran Foundation). The financial support by this organisation helps in transforming the lives of underprivileged students in society by contributing to their higher education.
Eligible: be domiciled in Karnataka
have passed the Class 10 or SSLC examination from Karnataka
have scored 90% or obtained a 9 CGPA in the Class 10 or SSLC examination (Note: The cut-off mark for students with disabilities is 75% or 7.5 CGPA.)
have an annual family income below ₹2,00,000 from all sources
note: The Sarojini Damodaran Foundation (SDF) will shortlist applicants based on academic merit and application details. Shortlisted candidates will then be invited for a short online test/interview
benefits: The selected students will receive a maximum of ₹10,000 per annum.
documents: A recent photograph
A scanned copy of Class 10 marksheet*
A scanned copy of the income certificate issued by a competent authority; ration card is not accepted
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.
Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and enter the required registration details. (Note - If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID)
Step 3: Navigate the 'Apply Now' at the bottom of the page and click on it.
Step 4: Click on 'Register' to start the application.
Step 5: Confirm the registration through the email sent by Vidhyadhan.
Step 6: Log in using the registered email ID and password.
Step 7: Click on the ‘Help’ link from the Vidyadhan Account for complete application instructions.
Step 8: Click on the ‘Application’ button to create a new application.
Step 9: Fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents and submit.

name: INDIAN INSTITUTES OF SCIENCE EDUCATION AND RESEARCH (IISERS) ADMISSIONS 2024
reward: Quality education, campus facility, medical facility and other benefits
eligibility: Class 12 or equivalent passed students
about: The Indian Institutes of Science Education and Research (IISERs) Admissions 2024 is the process of selecting students for the upcoming academic year (starting typically in July/August) across the seven IISER institutes in India. These institutes offer five-year BS-MS dual degree programs in various science disciplines. Admission to IISERs is done exclusively through the IISER Aptitude Test (IAT). It is a computer-based test that is scheduled to take place on Sunday, June 09, 2024 at 9:00 AM, at various centres across India. The computer-based test will be available in Hindi and English.
Eligible: Applicants must be Indian nationals, PIOs, or OCIs to apply for IAT 2024.
Candidates must have passed the Class 12 or equivalent level examination in 2022, 2023, or 2024 from any board recognised by the Council of Boards of School Education in India (COBSE).
Candidates appearing for the Class 12 or equivalent exam must have taken at least three of Biology, Chemistry, Mathematics, and Physics.
Candidates from SC/ST/PwD backgrounds need a minimum of 55% marks in Class 12, while candidates from other categories need a minimum of 60%.
note: Students from the Jammu and Kashmir Board of School Education (JKBOSE), specifically those from the Kashmir division, Winter Zone areas of Jammu division, and the U.T. of Ladakh, who appeared for their Class 12 or equivalent exam in December 2021, are also eligible to apply for IAT 2024, provided they meet all the other eligibility criteria.
benefits: Quality education, campus facility, medical facility and other benefits
documents: Marksheets/certificates
A valid passport
Caste certificate
Disability certificate (if applicable)
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click the ‘Apply Now’ button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and fill in the required details to 'Register'. (Note - If already registered, log in using the registered email ID.)
Step 3: Navigate to 'Aptitude Test' at the top of the dashboard and click on it for the drop-down menu.
Step 4: Click on 'Apply for IAT' in the last of the menu.
Step 5: Fill in the required information, upload the necessary documents, and submit.

name: MSIF MCDONALD FELLOWSHIP 2024
reward: Annual £30,000 for 2 years and additional £2,000 per year to the
eligibility: Early career multiple sclerosis researchers
about: MSIF McDonald Fellowship 2024 is an opportunity offered by the MSIF (Multiple Sclerosis International Federation)* to students pursuing M.Sc., Ph.D., and M.D. level studies in the field of multiple sclerosis. The fellowship enables early-career researchers in multiple sclerosis from low-income countries to work in a research institution outside their own country. Selected candidates will receive up to £30,000 (approximately ₹31,59,124.33) for 2 years and an additional grant of £2,000 (approximately ₹2,10,496.60) per year is provided to the host institution. The Multiple Sclerosis International Federation (MSIF) is a global organisation that brings together multiple sclerosis (MS) organisations, researchers, healthcare professionals and individuals affected by MS.
Eligible: be a citizen of a low- or middle-income country (including India)
be pursuing postgraduate studies, at least an M.Sc., preferably a Ph.D. or M.D. degree in an area relevant to multiple sclerosis
be working or studying in another country on a project supported by a Multiple Sclerosis (MS) International Federal grant that started within six months before the nomination
have focused their research on areas relevant to multiple sclerosis
note: Recipients of a Du Pré grant can subsequently apply for a McDonald Fellowship, but not the other way around.
benefits: The fellowship offers a two-year grant of around £30,000 (approximately ₹31,56,834.00) per year to cover travel and living costs. An additional contribution of £2,000 (approximately ₹2,10,496.60) per year is provided to the host institution. The fellowship expects part of this contribution in the second year to be used to cover the candidate's expenses to attend the annual congress of the European Committee for Treatment and Research (ECTRIMS) in MS.
documents: Previous class marksheet (if applicable)
A copy of passport (if applicable)
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click the ‘Apply Now’ button below.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and fill in the required details to 'Register'. (Note:- If already registered, log in using the registered email ID.)
Step 3: Navigate to the ‘How to Apply’ section and click the ‘Online Application Form’ link.
Step 4: Read the details carefully and fill in the required details to check the eligibility.
Step 5: After checking the eligibility, fill in the details, agree to the terms & conditions by clicking the checkbox and submit to 'Register' for the fellowship.

name: HSCST FELLOWSHIP PROGRAMME, HARYANA 2024
reward: A monthly fellowship of up to ₹35,000
eligibility: Students with M.Sc. degree or equivalent degree
about: HSCST Fellowship Programme 2024 is an opportunity offered by the Haryana State Council for Science, Innovation & Technology, to students who have completed their M.Sc. degree or hold an equivalent degree. This fellowship program aims to award research grants in Earth, Mathematical, Chemical, Physical, and Life Sciences. Selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend of up to ₹35,000  to support their full-time doctoral research at any accredited university or institution in Haryana.
Eligible: be under 28 years of age as of 1st June 2022
have completed an M.Sc. or equivalent degree
have secured a minimum of 55% marks
have qualified for the Joint CSIR-UGC NET exam for Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) and Lectureship (LS) held on 6th, 7th, and 8th June 2023
not be under any other fellowship/financial assistance from other sources
note: The age limit for Scheduled Castes/Scheduled Tribes, women, physically handicapped, and OBC applicants can be relaxed by 5 years.
This fellowship is open to candidates who have qualified for the JRF-NET (CSIR/UGC) exam but are not currently receiving CSIR/UGC funding for a valid reason.
benefits: For Junior Research Fellow (JRF): A monthly fellowship of ₹31,000. For Senior Research Fellow (SRF): A monthly fellowship of ₹35,000, For LS-NET qualified candidates: For Junior Research Fellow (JRF): A monthly fellowship of ₹18,000,For Senior Research Fellow (SRF): A monthly stipend of  ₹21,000. The fellowship includes an annual contingency grant of ₹20,000 which will be provided to the university or institution. The contingency grant will be provided pro-data for grants lasting less than one year. A part of this grant will be used for research work, purchasing books, etc. Any unspent balance of the contingency grant at the end of the year may be carried forward to the next year. The guidelines for utilising the contingency grant are provided in Annexure-II.
documents: Class 10 certificate as age proof
Applicant’s photograph
NET certificate
M.Sc. degree final year certificate
Caste certificate (if applicable)
Disability certificate (if applicable)

name: POGO-SCOR VISITING FELLOWSHIP PROGRAMME 2024
reward: A monthly stipend, International airfare, and other benefits
eligibility: Scientists, Technicians, postgraduate students
about: POGO-SCOR Visiting Fellowship Programme 2024 is an opportunity offered to scientists, technicians, postgraduates and post-doctoral fellows belonging to developing countries who have participated in economic transitions. This fellowship programme is jointly funded by the Partnership for Observation of the Global Ocean (POGO)* and the Scientific Committee on Oceanic Research (SCOR)**. It is designed to promote training and capacity building leading towards a global observation scheme for the oceans.
Eligible: be a citizen of India or any other developing country or economy in transition, as defined by the Development Assistance Committee (DAC) of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD)
be a scientist, technician, postgraduate student (Ph.D. preferred), or postdoctoral fellow working on oceanography
note: Priority will be given to early-career scientists.
All the documents must be uploaded in the PDF format.
Letters are to be uploaded along with the application and not sent directly to POGO email by the supervisors.
benefits: A monthly stipend, International airfare, Cost towards living expenses, Transportation costs from the airport to the host institute
documents: Fellowship proposal form (Size limit: 10MB)
Letter of Recommendation from the supervisor at the Parent Institution (Size limit: 5MB)
Letter of Acceptance from the supervisor at the Host Institution  (Size limit: 5MB)
Flight quotation evidence (Size limit: 9MB)

name: FOSSEE SUMMER FELLOWSHIP 2024
reward: A fellowship certificate and other benefits
eligibility: Graduate and postgraduate level students
about: FOSSEE Summer Fellowship 2025 is an opportunity provided by the FOSSEE project*. It is a hybrid fellowship where eligible students receive the chance to work with the FOSSEE team. Successful candidates will receive a certificate and recognition. The FOSSEE project (Free/Libre and Open Source Software for Education) aims to improve educational quality in India by promoting FLOSS tools and reducing reliance on proprietary software. It encourages the development and enhancement of solutions to meet academic and research needs and is part of the National Mission on Education through ICT under the Ministry of Education, Government of India.
Eligible: learn a Free/Libre and Open Source Software (FLOSS)
complete a minimum of one screening task from the list of tasks available
note: Please refer to the Fellowship Details document for the list of tasks and relevant information.
Fellows might receive an honorarium depending on their quality of work.
benefits: An opportunity to work with faculty from IIT Bombay, A certificate of FOSSEE summer fellowship, A recognition on the FOSSEE website

about: MoSPI Postgraduate/Research Students Internship 2024-25 is an opportunity offered by the Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MoSPI) to students pursuing postgraduate/research degrees in statistics. The main objective of this scheme is to create awareness amongst the students about the Indian Statistical Service and Subordinate Statistical Service. A total of 282 selected candidates will receive a monthly stipend of ₹5,000 for a duration of two months.

name: NHRC ONLINE SHORT-TERM INTERNSHIP PROGRAMME 2024
reward: A stipend of 2,000 and a certificate of participation
eligibility: Graduate and Postgraduate students
about: NHRC Online Short-Term Internship Programme 2025 is an initiative by the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) for graduate and postgraduate students. The Internship is spreading awareness among the students of the University about the need for the protection and promotion of Human Rights, the Commission has been conducting internship programs regularly since 1998. The selected interns will receive a stipend of ₹2,000 plus other benefits.
Eligible: not exceed the age of 28 years as of 1st February 2025
be pursuing studies in one of the following:
3rd year and onwards of an integrated 5 year PG course
3rd/final year of a graduation course
any semester/year of any postgraduate degree course (including 3-year LLB programs)
any semester/year of any postgraduate diploma course
research scholars in any stream
have secured a minimum of 60% marks in class 12 and all the examinations thereafter
note: The calculated percentage must be mentioned clearly from their CGPA/SGPA along with the formula for the percentage obtained. Failure to do so will result in the rejection of the application form.
Applicants with research potential in the fields of human rights will receive preference.
Interns of the previous NHRC internships are ineligible to apply again.
benefits: A stipend of ₹2,000 (one-time), A certificate of participation.
documents: Passport-sized photograph of the applicant (size not exceeding 100 KB)
Certificate of class 10
Certificate and mark sheet of class 12
Certificate and mark sheet of Graduation (if completed)
Mark sheet of the last qualifying examination
I-Card of currently pursuing course at the present institution
Recommendation letter of Principal/ Dean/ HoD from the institution where presently pursuing the course
250 words write-up on the topic: 'Statements of purpose for joining NHRC Online Short Term Internship'
Note:- Documents other than the photograph must not exceed the file size of 2 MB.

name: KSCSTE KERALA BACK-TO-LAB POST DOCTORAL FELLOWSHIP PROGRAMME 2024-25
reward: A monthly fellowship of 45,000 for up to two years and other
eligibility: Female scientists of Kerala
about: KSCSTE Kerala Back To Lab Post Doctoral Fellowship Programme 2024-25 is an opportunity offered by the Kerala State Council for Science, Technology and Environment (KSCSTE) to women who hold a Ph.D. degree in Science or Engineering and are permanently residing in Kerala. The program aims to support females who have taken breaks in their profession by offering post-doctoral fellowships and facilitating their re-entry into scientific research. The fellowship offers a monthly fellowship of ₹45,000 for two years and an annual contingent grant of ₹75,000, along with HRA for candidates living in rented accommodation during the project.
Eligible: be a female scientist permanently residing in Kerala
be aged under 40 years as of 1st February 2023
be a qualified Ph.D. degree holder in Science or Engineering as of the date of the application
have scored a minimum of 60% marks in postgraduation studies
be on a career break at the time of application because of obligations to family or other relevant issues
should not be receiving emoluments from any other sources
have at least one research paper published in SCI Journal/secured patent
note: The age limit can be relaxed by 3 years for OBC and 5 years for SC/ST applicants.
Applicants must not avail of a post-doctoral fellowship from KSCSTE or anywhere before.
Applicants should not be employed either on a permanent or temporary basis.
Applicants should choose an institution as a research center different from their Ph.D. research center.
Applicants should choose tenable institutions like R&D (national/state level) and University Departments in Kerala should belong to the category of Government Institutions.
Premier National Institutions viz., IISER, IIT, IISc. etc. outside the state will be considered under tenable institutions.
benefits: A monthly fellowship of ₹45,000 for a maximum of two years, An annual contingent grant of ₹75,000 for the duration of the project, House Rent Allowance (HRA) will be provided to those who are availing rented accommodation
documents: Photograph of applicant
Identity proof of applicant
Residency proof or domicile certificate
Postgraduation degree certificate and marksheet
Ph.D. certificate
Work and research experience certificate
Reprints of latest Research Publications in SCI-indexed journals (maximum 2 pages)
Break in Career certificate (if applicable)
Declaration by applicant
Consent from a scientist mentor
Endorsement from the head of the institution
Biodata the applicant including the list of publications (not more than 2 pages)
Biodata of scientist mentor (not more than 2 pages)
Date of Birth proof (Note:- Applicants seeking age relaxation due to reservation category should submit a combined document as proof.)

Step 7: Accept the terms and click on ‘Go’.
Step 8: Fill in all the required information, upload the necessary documents and submit the application.

name: SWAMI VIVEKANANDA SCHOLARSHIP FOR ACADEMIC EXCELLENCE SCHEME 2024
reward: Living expenses of up to 1,00,000 per month
eligibility: Undergraduate, Postgraduate, Ph.D. and Postdoctoral students
about: Swami Vivekananda Scholarship for Academic Excellence Scheme 2024 is an initiative of the Government of Rajasthan, for students planning to pursue undergraduate, postgraduate, Ph.D., and post-doctoral research programs abroad. Formerly known as the Rajiv Gandhi Scholarship for Academic Excellence, this scheme aims to provide scholarships to residents of Rajasthan pursuing higher studies in any subject/course at a top-ranked university/institution. This includes the top 150 universities globally according to the latest QS World Rankings and the top 50 institutions in India. Selected students will receive full tuition fees, including bench fees (if applicable), and a stipend for living expenses.
Eligible: be a citizen of India
be a resident of Rajasthan
be less than 35 years of age as of 1st July of the financial year related to scholarship allocation
be enrolled in an undergraduate, postgraduate, Ph.D. or postdoctoral degree

have received an offer letter/confirmation letter/admission letter from any of the universities/institutions listed in the latest QS Global Rankings or ranked within the top 1-50 overall in NIRF institutions
meet the following annual income criteria:
Less than ₹8,00,000 (E1 category)
Between ₹8,00,000 and INR 25,00,000 (E2 category)
More than ₹25,00,000 (E3 category)
note: E1, E2, and E3 define distinct income brackets that determine the level of financial assistance awarded.
Provisionally selected scholarship candidates must submit unconditional offer letters or admission letters within two months of their provisional selection.
However, the scholarship eligibility will not be affected by financial details in the offer letter, such as fee payment requirements, other charges, or proof of funding.
benefits: Living expenses, including annual maintenance allowance, annual incidental and equipment allowance, medical insurance premium, accommodation, TCS (if applicable), visa fees, airfare (economy class) for travel abroad and return to India, a laptop, and advance payment for other one-time expenses, will be covered for the duration of the course
documents: Aadhaar Number (if applicable)
Passport (if applicable)
Visa (if applicable)
Offer letter from the relevant foreign university
Domicile certificate (shortened 'Bonafide/domicile certificate')
Gross family income certificate (authenticated by an executive magistrate and witnessed by two gazetted officers)
Income Tax Returns (ITRs) for the financial year 2023-24 (applicable for applications submitted in the financial year 2024-25)
Affidavit for not filing ITR (in the provided format)
Marksheet/certificate of the last passed examination
Self-declaration for scholarships/financial aid received from other sources (if applicable)

Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note - If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID.)

Step 5: Click on 'Jan Aadhaar' or 'Google' to 'Register'.
Step 6: Fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents and submit.

name: AICHI SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM 2024
reward: A living allowance of ¥1,50,000 per month and other benefits
eligibility: Master's degree applicants
about: Aichi Scholarship Program 2024 is an opportunity offered by the Aichi Prefectural Government to Indian students who want to pursue their postgraduate studies in Japan. The primary objective of this scholarship is to support the students pursuing fields relevant to Aichi's manufacturing industry. 5 scholarships are available, each offering a monthly stipend of ¥150,000 (approximately ₹80,767.72) to cover living expenses.
Eligible: be under 35 years old as of 1st April 2024 i.e. born on or after 2nd April 1988
be citizen of and currently reside in India or one of the United Nations member states excluding Japan and North Korea, Vatican City, Kosovo, Cook Islands, and Niue
hold a bachelor's degree or equivalent qualification
select a field of study relevant to Aichi's manufacturing industry
demonstrate proficiency in Japanese or a clear plan to achieve proficiency for study and potential employment in Aichi
be in good physical and mental health to participate fully in the program
note: Spouses of individuals currently receiving scholarships from the Aichi Prefectural Government are ineligible to apply
Applicants must intend to seek employment at a company with headquarters or a branch office located in Aichi regardless of the work placement location within Japan
Applicants cannot be receiving scholarships or grants concurrently from other organizations, including those from the Japanese government or their home country
Airfare for return flights from Japan to the home country will not be provided
The monthly living allowance of ¥1,50,000 may be reduced in the following situations: Leaving Japan for more than 30 days; Taking a leave of absence from school

The allowance may be reduced to ¥100,000 per month starting in April 2026 if the recipient has not passed the N4 or a higher level of the Japanese Language Proficiency Test (JLPT) by the end of March 2026
If the recipient passes the N4 or a higher JLPT level and submits the certificate, the allowance will be reinstated to ¥150,000 per month starting the following month
In exceptional circumstances where taking the JLPT is impossible, alternative proficiency assessments will be considered
benefits: A living allowance of ¥1,50,000 per month which will be paid from the month of arrival in Japan, Travel expenses will cover economy class airfare to Japan approved by the Governor within the program budget. This will include travel from the nearest international airport to the students' Chubu Centrair International Airport residence. In exceptional circumstances where these airports cannot be used, the Governor may approve necessary travel expenses.School expenses such as entrance fees, exam fees, tuition fees for both research and graduate study
documents: Application Form (Form 1, completed and signed)
Pledge (Form 2, completed and signed)

name: UK-INDIA TOEFL SCHOLARSHIP 2024
reward: A one-time scholarship of ₹2,50,000
eligibility: Students entolled in undergraduate or postgraduate
about: UK-India TOEFL Scholarship 2024 is a program that supports Indian students who are pursuing full-time undergraduate or postgraduate studies in the UK. Offered by the Educational Testing Service (ETS), in collaboration with the National Indian Students and Alumni Union (NISAU), the scholarship programme provides a great opportunity for Indian students to offset the cost of their education in the UK. The award amount is ₹2,50,000 each for 10 selected students.
Eligible: Applicants must be Indian citizens with valid Indian passports.
Applicants must have taken the TOEFL iBT test between 1st April and 15th July 2024 and scored a minimum of 75 out of a total of 120 marks.
Students must have an admission offer letter for a full-time on-campus undergraduate or postgraduate program in the UK university for the intake during 2024 Fall or 2025 Spring.
note: Meeting eligibility requirements does not guarantee receiving the scholarship.

All application information submitted by an applicant, including TOEFL scores and other details, must be accurate and complete. ETS India reserves the right to reject any application in its entirety if provided information is found to be inaccurate, misleading, or unverifiable.

ETS India reserves the right to withdraw the scholarship if the recipient is found to be ineligible or fails to comply with the given terms and conditions or make amendments to the scholarship provisions at any time without prior notice.
Successful recipient shall inform ETS India of any other scholarship award being received prior or post the date of award of the UK-India TOEFL Scholarship.

If a candidate qualifies for multiple scholarships, awards, or prizes offered by ETS between April 2024 and September 2024, they are eligible to receive only one. The award with the highest value will be granted.

Scholarship recipients are expected to participate as the global alumni ambassadors after having completed their graduation. Upon request by ETS India, they will have to share their experiences with prospective applicants on the pre-scholarship and post-scholarship journey.

Scholarship recipients who withdraw from their course before completion may be required to repay all or part of the scholarship award as per the discretion of ETS India. Medical exceptions along with supporting documentation will be taken into consideration.
ETS India's responsibility is limited to disbursing the scholarship award (in ₹) to the recipient's designated bank account of the recipient.
ETS India reserves the final decision on scholarship awards. These decisions are binding on all applicants.
benefits: Up to 10 Indian students will be selected to receive one-time scholarship of ₹2,50,000 each. These funds are intended to provide financial support for college expenses, including but not limited to expenditures on tuition, books, deposits for accommodation and transportation. As a benefit of receiving the UK-India TOEFL Scholarship, recipients will also serve as TOEFL Student Ambassadors for the duration of their studies. This may involve: representing TOEFL as a Student Ambassador at university fairs, educational workshops, or other relevant eventsdeveloping content for marketing and promotional activities, such as writing blog posts or creating video testimonials about their experiences participating in relevant international events for students engagement as a valued member of the global alumni network under the TOEFL program
documents: A copy of a valid passport
Bank details and a cancelled cheque
PAN card details
Academic transcripts, including marksheets and certificates
An essay on the topic ‘Creating a Social Impact with a UK-based Education' in 500 words
Highlight of personal & academic achievements

name: ASEM-DUO SWEDEN FELLOWSHIP PROGRAM 2024
reward: A fellowship of up to €4000 for a semester
eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students
about: ASEM-DUO Sweden Fellowship Program 2024 is an initiative of the Asia-Europe Meeting (ASEM) and is sponsored by the Swedish Council for Higher Education (UHR). It offers financial support for exchange semesters between undergraduate and graduate students from Asian countries and Sweden.  Students travel in pairs, with one student from Sweden and one student from an Asian ASEM member country. The program promotes balanced academic exchanges and cultural understanding between Sweden and Asian countries. Selected students can receive a fellowship of up to €4000 for a semester for a single semester.
Eligible: be a citizen of one of the 21 ASEM member countries in Asia including India, Bangladesh, China, Indonesia, Japan, Korea, Mongolia, Philippines, Thailand, and Vietnam
hold a valid passport
be enrolled in an Asian university (host institution) that has a pre-existing exchange agreement with a Swedish university (home institution)
note: A Swedish university can nominate an EU student enrolled at its institution to study at its Asian partner university
The Asian university is then obligated to accept this nomination
In reciprocity, the Asian university can nominate a student enrolled at its institution to study at the Swedish university
the Swedish university must accept this nomination
benefits: In high-income countries: €3,500 per pair (Asian student + Swedish student), In middle/low-income countries: €4,000 for the Asian student and €3,000 for the Swedish student
documents: A duly filled online application form
A copy of the applicant's passport (biographical page recommended)
Latest academic transcripts including marksheets and certificates
A copy of the cooperation agreement or Memorandum of Understanding for cooperation between paired institutions OR
A declaration of intention to set up a new cooperation agreement, signed by the Head of Swedish Higher Education Institution

2. Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID.)

3. Swedish educational institutions apply on behalf of both institutions and participants in the exchange program. Applicants interested in participating should contact the relevant offices (e.g., International Relations Offices) at their educational institutions to apply.

In its 8th edition, the program seeks to inspire undergraduate engineering students by recognising their creativity, innovation, leadership, and excellence. How_to_apply: Stage 1: Online Application & Evaluation,
Only candidates who clear the eligibility criteria set out by the IET India Scholarship Award will be considered for the Online Application round.
Shortlisted candidates will be invited to take an online test on STEM (Science, Technology, Engineering and Mathematics) essentials.
The shortlisted candidates from Stage 1 will advance to Stage 2, which will include the top 10 percentile of applicants from every region (North, East & Northeast, South 1, South 2, and West).

South 2: Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh and Pondicherry
The scores will be calculated from the total applications received, based on the weightages assigned to each of the questions in the online application form.

have passed the Class 10/SSC examination in 2024 from a recognised school/institution of the state

name: BUDDY4STUDY - PRODIGY FINANCE EDUCATION LOAN PROGRAM
reward: Flexible international student loans
eligibility: For college students who aspire to study abroad
about: Buddy4Study - Prodigy Finance Education Loan Program is a joint initiative of Buddy4Study and Prodigy Finance that provides financial backing to aspiring students who have secured admission into higher educational institutions abroad.This loan program distinguishes itself by assessing applicants' affordability based on their future earning potential rather than their current circumstances. Additionally, this program offers extensive support for students from diverse backgrounds. The average loan size changes depending on what applicants state in their loan applications. The program's smallest loan is $10,001, and the typical loan is $36,000 for each academic period
Eligible: They must be accepted into a school and program supported by Prodigy Finance. Applicants can apply for a quote before receiving their acceptance letter; proof of admission is required only when finalising the loan.
Applicants must be planning to study abroad.
They must reside in one of the countries supported by Prodigy Finance. (List of unsupported countries)
note: If these criteria are met, applicants can complete a quick online application to receive a quote. In most cases, the quote will be provided immediately, indicating the eligible loan amount, interest rate, loan term, and any applicable fees.
If attending a school in the US: Prodigy Finance can provide funding for students living in the US, US citizens, and international students outside the US.
If attending a school in the UK: Funding is available for UK residents, citizens, and international students outside the UK.
If attending a school in France: Prodigy Finance cannot offer funding for students living in France who also plan to study at a French university.

If attending a school elsewhere: Funding is available for students who have not resided or worked in their study country for more than 90 days before beginning their studies. This excludes France but includes Canadian provinces and territories.
Students studying in certain US states like Rhode Island and Vermont may not be eligible for funding.
benefits: Buddy4Study - Prodigy Finance Education Loan Program offers loans with varying amounts, depending on the information provided in the loan application. The program's smallest loan is $10,001, while the average loan per academic period is $36,000. The maximum loan amount depends on the school's determined cost of attendance. In some situations, Prodigy Finance may offer funding for the entire program in one application. In other cases, applicants may need to apply separately for each academic period. For example, a student might borrow $45,000 for the fall semester and an additional $15,000 for the spring semester. Prodigy Finance charges simple interest on loans, meaning that interest doesn't compound over the loan's lifespan. Prodigy Finance doesn't gain from increasing interest rates. They levy a processing fee of up to $100 to reserve funds and a fixed administrative fee of up to 5% of the loan amount to finalise the loan. These fees cover operational expenses.
documents: Identity Verification: Applicants are asked to upload a clear, colour scan of the identification page of their valid passport. Additionally, they must provide a selfie to match the photo in their passport.

Proof of Address: A document displaying the applicant's full name and residential address, dated within the last three months, must be submitted. The address provided in the application should match the document.

School Admission Confirmation: Applicants must provide a letter, I-20, or email from their school confirming their acceptance. This document should clearly state the applicant's name, school, and course details and indicate their admission status.

Credit Report: Applicants must provide a copy of their credit report from a credit reference agency in their country. If they lack a credit history, they can submit a 'No hit' or 'No data' report instead.
How_to_apply: Click the ‘Apply Now’ button below
Login to Buddy4Study with your registered ID and land on the ‘Application Form Page’
Register at Buddy4Study with your email/mobile number/Gmail account if not registered
You will now be redirected to the ‘Buddy4Study - Prodigy Finance Education Loan Program’ application form page
Click on the ‘Start Application’ button to begin the application process
Fill in the required details in the online application form
Upload relevant documents
Accept the ‘Terms and Conditions’ and click on ‘Preview’
If all the details filled in by the applicant are correctly showing on the preview screen, click on the ‘Submit’ button to complete the application process

name: COMMON ENTRANCE TEST (CET) 2024
reward: N/A
eligibility: Professional degree courses applicants
about: The Common Entrance Test (CET-2024) serves as the common platform for the admission process meant for selective bachelor's degree programs offered by the following institutes under the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DEPwD), Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India: Swami Vivekanand National Institute of Rehabilitation Training and Research (SVNIRTAR), Cuttack National Institute for Locomotor Disabilities (NILM), Kolkata National Institute for Empowerment of Persons with Multiple Disabilities (NIEPMD), Chennai Pandit Deendayal Upadhyaya National Institute for Persons with Physical Disabilities (PDUNIPPD), New Delhi Composite Regional Centre for Skill Development Rehabilitation & Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (CRCSRE), Guwahati Established in the early 1980s under the Department of Empowerment of Persons with Disabilities (DIVYANGJAN), Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India, these Institutes function as autonomous bodies. Their primary aims and objectives are outlined below: Human Resource Development in the field of rehabilitation of PwDs.Developing a model service delivery system for people with disabilities.Undertaking research and development in the field of rehabilitation.Undertaking consultancy services.Undertaking extension & outreach programs These Institutes provide training programs in their respective areas of specialisation at the certificate, diploma, degree, and postgraduate levels. These institutes are also actively involved in research, and development of innovative models for rehabilitation services, manpower development, and clinical and therapeutic interventions. Additionally, they maintain extensive outreach programs to ensure decentralised availability of these services. Notably, some of these Institutes offer specialised courses that require candidates to qualify for an All-India Common Entrance Test.
Eligible: Applicants should be Indian citizens or citizens of a foreign country, particularly from neighbouring Southeast Asian countries
Students must be 17 years old at the time of admission or should be 17 years old on or before 31st December in the year of admission to the first year of the BPT/BOT/BPO program
(Note:- There is no age limit criteria applicable to students pursuing a Bachelor in Audiology and Speech-Language Pathology (BASLP) course.)
Candidates who have appeared for the Class 12 examination (Qualifying) but are awaiting results in their essential subjects can apply and appear for the Common Entrance Test 2024
These candidates must upload their 10+2 marksheet after the result is declared and published
note: The entrance test exam and all subsequent instructions will be published in English language only
Candidates selected for the course will be required to deposit the course fee and caution money according to the norms of the institute
If a candidate withdraws from the course mid-year, for any reason, the course fee (except for the caution money) will be forfeited
However, the caution money will be refunded
A stipend may be awarded during the internship period according to the prevailing rules at the time of award
The institute reserves the right to reject an incomplete application form
No attempt to influence the results of CET-2024 through correspondence or canvassing in any form will be tolerated
The institute is not responsible for applications or enclosures not received by the notified deadline, regardless of the reason
Candidates are expected to follow the rules of discipline and code of conduct established by the institutes carefully
Ragging in any form is strictly prohibited
Violators will face severe disciplinary action from the institute, which may include initiation of legal proceedings under IPC and Cr. PC
Hostel allotment is subject to availability each year and may depend on disciplinary conduct and/or academic performance
Students admitted to the hostel will be required to follow the established hostel rules
They should maintain the utmost discipline, decency, and decorum in their interactions with staff, fellow residents, and students
Keeping bicycles and cars in the hostel is not permitted
No student will stay in the hostel beyond the duration of the course in which they have enrolled
Students must attend classes regularly
Attendance should be maintained as per the respective norms of the university
All legal matters related to CET-2024 will be subject to the jurisdiction of the Honourable High Court of the designated state
Candidates can submit only one application form
Any attempt to submit multiple applications, whether for the same stream or different streams, will result in the rejection of all applications
benefits: Selected students will get admissions to the specified institutes.
documents: Applicant's photograph with white background [in JPEG/JPG format and size from 40-100 KB and (100x100) pixels]
Applicant's signature [in JPEG/JPG format and size from 40-50KB and (200x100) pixels]
Applicant's thumb impression [in JPEG/JPG format and size from 40-100KB and (100x100) pixels]
Caste certificate (if applicable)*
Academic transcripts including marksheets and certificates (PDF format and size up to 300 KB)
PwD/PH certificate (and UDID card, if applicable)
EWS certificate issued by a competent authority within one year before the counselling date
Residence/domicile/nativity certificate (For candidates under Northeast and Jammu & Kashmir reservation categories)
Class 10+2 science marksheet (required if passed) [Note:- This document is not applicable for candidates who have appeared for the qualifying examination but haven't received their results yet.]
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button and fill in the required details to register. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile Number/Email ID.)
Step 3: Visit the official website of the respective institutions, look for the 'Common Entrance Test (CET)' section and click on the 'Apply' button.

Step 4: Fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents, and make an online non-refundable payment of examination fees through a debit card/credit card internet banking/UPI/wallet using online gateway payment facility. BillDeskgateway is also available for online transactions. (Note:- BillDesk Gateway transaction charges will be applicable as per the rates in effect at the time of application submission.)
Step 5: Submit the application form.

For this BCA program, Eternal University will confer the degree course. NavGurukul will serve as the pedagogy partner, bringing its unique teaching methods and a strong focus on placements. Sri Badrika Ashram, also serving as a program partner, will provide financial support.

Students must be willing to secure admission to the BCA program at the university.
The annual family income must not be more than ₹6,00,000.
note: There is no registration fee applicable for students at the time of application.
Selected applicants who accept the offer for admission under the three-year residential BCA programme will incur a one-time only cost of ₹23,500 for the registration, uniform and exam fees.
There shall be no more fees applicable for the next 3 years.
Other benefits include free food, accommodation, and a laptop for the course duration.
benefits: The selected candidates will receive a waiver of ₹3.25 lakhs towards tuition fees, accommodation, food, and a laptop for the course duration.Upon successful completion of the BCA program, 100% placement assistance will be provided to the students. about: University of Exeter India Global Leaders Scholarship 2024-25 is an opportunity offered by the University of Exeter to Indian citizens who are enrolled in a campus-based master's programme at the university. The main objective of this scholarship program is to ensure diversity in the academic and cultural profile by including students from India which will help enrich and contribute to every aspect of their master's programme. Selected candidates will receive a tuition fee reduction of worth £3,000 (approximately ₹3,14,21.41).
Eligible: be an Indian citizen with a valid passport.
be classed as an International student for fee purposes.
hold an offer to study in one of the following campus-based masters degree programmes at the University of Exeter commencing in September 2024:
The Exeter MBA,
MRes Global Political Economy,
MRes Management,
MRes Economics (Pathway to PhD),
MSc Human Resources Management (24 or 30 months),
MSc Minerals Processing (e.g., Professional Conversion),
MSc Mining Engineering (e.g., Professional Practice),
MSc Island Biodiversity and Conservation,
Legal Practice Course (LPC),
Graduate Diploma in Law (GDL),
MA Law (SQE1); MA Law (Conversion),
LLM Legal Practice (SQE 1&2),
SQE Preparation Courses,
Clinical Psychology programmes delivered by Clinical Education, Development and Research (CEDAR),
Master of Public Administration (MPA) with Applied Study,
Postgraduate diplomas,
Degree apprenticeships.
meet the conditions of that offer as set out in their programme offer letter.
note: Applicants who have received their offer of admission prior to March 2024 must pay the tuition fee deposit by the deadline stated in the scholarship communication letter.
Applicants who have received their offer of admission from March 2024 onwards must pay the tuition fee deposit by the deadline stated in the original program offer letter
benefits: Selected candidates will receive a tuition fee reduction of worth £3,000 (approximately ₹3,14,21.41).
documents: A valid passport
Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV)
Academic transcripts including marksheets, certificates, etc.
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.
Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID.)

Step 3: This grant will be automatically awarded to eligible students applying for admission in September 2024 who have paid their deposit by the previously stated deadlines. A separate application is not required.

name: SOF GIRL CHILD SCHOLARSHIP SCHEME (G.C.S.S) 2024-25
reward: 5,000 per annum (one-time)
eligibility: Female students of Class 1 to 10
about: SOF Girl Child Scholarship Scheme (G.C.S.S) 2024-25 is an opportunity provided by the Science Olympiad Foundation (SOF)* for female students studying in Class 1 to 10. The scholarship aims to provide financial assistance to students from economically disadvantaged families so they can continue their school education. A total of 300 female students will be awarded a one-time scholarship of ₹5,000 each. SOF is an educational organisation established to promote Science, Mathematics, Computer Education, English, Social Studies, General Knowledge and professional courses.
Eligible: be a female currently studying in Class 1 to 10 in a recognised school/institution.
have secured at least 60% marks or equivalent in the previous academic year.
have a monthly family income of less than ₹15,000.
note: Marks criteria can be waived for physically disabled female students.
benefits: 300 selected girl children across India will receive ₹5,000 each annually.
documents: A signed copy of the previous year's report card from the recommending school's principal
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.
Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note - If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID)
Step 3: The students are requested to contact the school to apply for the opportunity.

name: BRIGHT MINDS SCHOLARSHIP 2024-25
reward: Up to 100% scholarship worth up to 6 lakh
eligibility: For admission to B.Tech. or MBA programs at institutions
about: The Bright Minds Scholarship is a merit-based initiative by Sunstone that offers up to 100% scholarship worth ₹6 lakh to students willing to enrol in the first year of B.Tech. and MBA courses at institutions powered by Sunstone. While B.Tech. aspirants can claim the scholarship based on their JEE Mains score, the MBA aspirants can claim the scholarship amount based on their CAT/XAT percentile.
Eligible: Open for students enrolling in their first-year of B.Tech. and MBA programs at colleges powered by Sunstone.
Students can take admission to the following colleges through Sunstone to avail this Scholarship:
Shri Ramswaroop Memorial University, Lucknow - (For B.Tech program)
GD Goenka University, Gurgaon - (For MBA and B.Tech program)
Ajeenkya DY Patil University, Pune - (For MBA program)
Students enrolling in B.Tech programs must have scored more than 80% marks in their Class 12 examination or at least 75 percentile in their JEE (Mains) score.
Students enrolling in MBA programs must have obtained at least a 75 percentile score in CMAT/CAT examination.
note: Either the Class 12 percentage or JEE percentile will be used to determine eligibility.
benefits: empowers college students with a recruiter-driven curriculum, real-world projects and internships, allowing them to practise what they have learned.
documents: Government-issued address proof (e.g., Aadhaar card)
Copy of Class 12/graduation marksheet
Copy of JEE Mains/CAT/XAT scorecard
How_to_apply: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below.
Login to Buddy4Study using a registered ID to land on the 'Online Application Form Page.'
If not registered - Register at Buddy4Study with your Email/Mobile number/Gmail account.
You will now be redirected to the ‘Bright Minds Scholarship 2023-24' application form page.
Fill in the required details in the online application form.
Upload the required documents.
Submit the application process.

name: H.H DALAI LAMA-SASAKAWA SCHOLARSHIP 2024-25
reward: Tuition fees, admission fee, mess fee and boarding/hostel
eligibility: Students residing in self-settled leprosy colonies in specified
about: H.H Dalai Lama-Sasakawa Scholarship 2024-25 is an opportunity offered by the Sasakawa India Leprosy Foundation (SILF) to provide financial support to students residing in self-settled leprosy colonies in Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Delhi, Jharkhand, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal, Telangana and Andhra Pradesh. The scholarship will be given to students who are willing to pursue professional courses in recognised institutions in the form of tuition fees, admission fees, mess fees and boarding/ hostel expenses.
Eligible: not be less than 17 years of age at the time of application.
be a domicile of one of the following states: Andhra Pradesh, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Delhi, Jharkhand, Madhya Pradesh, Odisha, Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal, Telangana.
have at least one parent who has leprosy.
be residing in a self-settled leprosy colony.
be enrolled in a professional course at a government recognised institution.
have completed senior secondary education or bachelor's degree in the year 2023-22.
note: Students enrolled in non-professional courses are not eligible.
benefits: The selected candidates will receive tuition fees, admission fees, mess fees and boarding/hostel expenses from the institutes directly.
documents: Passport-size photographs of the applicant.
Self-attested copies of the following documents:
Birth certificate or proof of date of birth.
Pass Certificate for matriculation (Class 10), senior secondary (Class 12), and Graduation (if applying for a master’s course).
Class 10, 12 board exam and graduation marksheets (if applying for master’s course).
Leprosy certificate of parent(s) (treatment certificate from the place where treated or certificate from District Leprosy Officer or disability certificate mentioning Hansen’s disease/leprosy).
A provisional admission letter issued by the institute to which the applicant has secured admission, or else the applicant should indicate the names of 3 colleges in which they are seeking admission.
A reference letter from the colony leader certifying that the candidate is a resident of the colony and is the only candidate from their family.
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button below and read all details.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and fill in the required details to 'Register'. (Note - If already registered, log in using the registered email ID.)
Step 3: Navigate to 'Click here to Apply for HHDLS Scholarship 2024 - 25' on the left-hand side of the page and click on the given link.
Step 4: Now, click on 'If you do not have a login account click here to register' and fill in the details to 'Register'.
Step 5: Fill in the required details, attach the relevant documents, and submit.

name: AHIMSA FELLOWSHIP 2024
reward: A monthly stipend of up to 20,000 and other benefits
eligibility: Bachelor's degree holders
about: The Ahimsa Fellowship 2024 is a 8-month full-time opportunity for graduates designed to create a network of effective animal protection leaders. Through this programme, fellows will gain the skills and knowledge to collaborate closely with state governments and district administrations to enforce animal protection laws in India. It seeks trained professionals who can capably assist the Government in fulfilling its statutory duties through systemic apparatuses like State Animal Welfare Boards, Societies For The Prevention of Cruelty to Animals, Infirmaries, Slaughterhouse Monitoring Committees, Animal Birth Control Monitoring Committees etc. Successful applicants will receive a competitive monthly stipend of up to ₹20,000 along with other valuable benefits.
Eligible: Be an Indian citizen
Hold a bachelor's degree in any discipline from a recognised institution/university/college
Be between 21 to 39 years of age as of 31 December 2024
Possess strong communication skills in English and a regional language
Be available to attend the 8-month duration of the fellowship programme
note: Once the documentation is finalised, the selected candidates secure a spot in Cohort 4 (2024–25) of the Ahimsa Fellowship
benefits: For the first two months of the 8-month program, fellows will receive a stipend of ₹10,000 per month. Thereafter, a monthly stipend of ₹20,000. The stipend is designed to cover expenses related to completing program assignments. Travel and accommodation for the residential contact classes will be covered by Team Ahimsa Fellowship and is NOT to be borne by the candidate. Out-of-pocket expenses beyond the stipend may be reimbursed on a case-by-case basis.
documents: Stage 1 - Evaluation of the online application form.
Stage 2 - Applicants who have cleared stage 1 will be invited for a virtual interview.
Stage 3 - Online assessment will be provided to candidates who clear the interview.
Stage 4 - Candidates who have performed well in the assessment will proceed to the panel interview.
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.
Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and fill in the required registration details. (Note:- If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID)
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Apply Now' button and click on it.
Step 4: Click on the 'Applicant/Fellow Sign-Up' tab.
Step 5: Fill in the required details, upload the photograph and submit.
Step 6: Once registered, click on the 'Applicant/Fellow Login' tab.
Step 7: Enter Username, password and login.
Step 8: Fill in the application form consisting of 5 sub-sections (including personal details).
Step 9: Once the applicant has completed filling out the entire form, lock your form and submit it.

name: VIDYADHAN LADAKH CLASS 11 SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM 2024
reward: 10,000 per annum
eligibility: Class 10 passed students
about: Vidyadhan Ladakh Class 11 Scholarship Program 2024 is an opportunity offered by the Sarojini Damodaran Foundation to Class 11 and 12 students of the union territory. Established in 1999 by SD Shibulal (Co-founder and former CEO, Infosys) and Kumari Shibulal, (Sarojini Damodaran Foundation), this financial support helps to transform the lives of the underprivileged students in society by contributing to their higher education. The selected candidates will receive ₹10,000 per annum.
Eligible: Be domiciled in the Union Territory of Ladakh.
Have completed the Class 10/SSC exam in the year 2024 from a recognised school/institute of the Union Territory.
Have scored 60% or obtained a 6 CGPA in the Class 10/SSC examination. (Note: The cut-off mark for students with disability is 55% or 5.5 CGPA.)
Have an annual family income of less than ₹2,00,000 from all sources.
note: Applicants missing their original marksheet can upload a provisional/online version from the SSLC/CBSE/ICSE website.
benefits: The selected candidates will receive ₹10,000 per annum.
documents: A recent passport-size photograph
A scanned copy of the Class 10 marksheet*
A scanned copy of the income certificate issued by a competent authority (Note: Ration card is not accepted)
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.
Step 2: Click the 'Register' button and enter the required registration details. (Note: If already registered, log in using Gmail/Mobile number/Email ID.)
Step 3: Navigate to the 'Apply Now' at the bottom of the page and click on it.
Step 4: Click on 'Register' to start the application.
Step 5: Confirm the registration through the email sent by Vidhyadhan.
Step 6: Log in using the registered email ID and password.
Step 7: Click on the Help link from the Vidhyadhan Account for complete application instructions.
Step 8: Click on the Application button to create a new application.
Step 9: Fill in the required details, upload the necessary documents, and submit.

name: FULBRIGHT KALAM CLIMATE FELLOWSHIP FOR ACADEMIC AND PROFESSIONAL EXCELLENCE 2025-26
reward: A monthly stipend, J-1 visa support, and other benefits
eligibility: Indian faculty, researchers, and professionals
about: Fulbright Kalam Climate Fellowship for Academic and Professional Excellence 2025-26 is an opportunity offered by the United States-India Educational Foundation (USIEF), that empowers Indian faculty and researchers to teach, conduct research, or both, at a U.S. institution. The selected candidates will receive a monthly stipend, J-1 visa support, and other benefits.
Eligible: be a faculty member or researcher
hold a Ph.D. degree with at least five years of relevant teaching, research, or professional experience OR
hold a master's degree with recognised professional standing and at least five years of relevant experience
follow the employer endorsement instructions, if currently employed
note: The name must be entered exactly the way it appears (or will appear) on the applicant's passport.
benefits: Dependent allowances and international travel may be provided, subject to funding availability, for one eligible accompanying dependent who resides with the grantee in the U.S. for at least 80% of the fellowship period.
documents: Curriculum vitae (CV)
Study/research objectives
A recent publication (copy of paper/article not exceeding 30 pages)
Employers endorsement form
Ph.D. registration certificate
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.
Step 2: Click on the 'Register' button on the right-hand side of the dashboard and fill in the required details to 'Register'. (Note - If already registered, log in using the registered email Id.)
Step 3: Navigate to the 'How to Apply' section and click on the first link.
Step 4: For first-time users, click on the 'Create an Account' link, whereas registered users click on Login.
Step 5: To register for an account, enter an email address, first and last name, and date of birth and click on 'Continue'.
Step 6: Enter the pin received through email to activate the account and create a password to complete the process.

Step 7: After successfully creating the account, fill in the required information and upload the necessary documents in PDF format as required. (Note- Applicants who already have an account can log in using valid credentials)
Step 9: Fill in the FKAPE Applicant Annexure and FKAPE Letter of Support from Home Institute, and submit the form.

reward: A monthly stipend and other benefits

about: Fulbright-Kalam Climate Fellowship for Postdoctoral Research (2025-26), offered by the United States-India Educational Foundation (USIEF), to Indian researchers in the early stages of their careers. This fellowship program aims to strengthen their research capabilities by providing them with research opportunities in the United States. Fellows will gain access to top-notch resources in their fields and foster long-term collaborative relationships with US faculty and researchers. The selected fellows will receive a monthly stipend and other benefits.
Eligible: be an Indian national
be registered for a Ph.D. degree at an Indian institution on or before 1st November 2023
have a publication in a reputable peer-reviewed journal
demonstrate a strong record of academic and professional achievement
have done reasonable study pertaining to their research objectives, especially in the identification of resources in India and the U.S
note: These fellowships are intended for doctoral students in the early stages of their research. Applicants who have completed their Ph.D. or are nearing thesis submission are not eligible.
benefits: A monthly stipend, J1 visa support, Accident and sickness program for exchanges per U.S. Government guidelines, round-trip economy class air travel, A modest settling-in allowance, A professional allowance
documents: Curriculum vitae (CV)
Study/research objectives
A recent publication (copy of paper/article not exceeding 30 pages)
Employers endorsement form
Ph.D. registration certificate
How_to_apply: Step 1: Click on the 'Apply Now' button.

Step 4: For first-time users, click on the 'Create an Account' link, whereas registered users click on Login.

name: ESRI INDIA MASTER'S SCHOLARSHIP PROGRAM 2024
reward: An annual scholarship award of 000'00′12

about: Esri India Master's Scholarship Program 2024 is an opportunity offered by Esri India to students starting their second year of a postgraduate (M.Tech./M.Sc.) program in Geoinformatics or a related field that involves studying remote sensing, GIS, spatial modeling, spatial analysis, digital image processing, and similar subjects. A total of 10 selected students will receive an annual scholarship of ₹1,00,000. Established in 1996, Esri India Technologies Pvt. Ltd. (Esri India) is an end-to-end Geographic Information Systems (GIS) solutions provider. They offer a comprehensive suite of tools under the name ArcGIS, which is a powerful mapping and location analytics platform.
Eligible: be at least 18 years old
be a resident of India
be enrolled in the beginning of the second year of a postgraduate (M.Tech./M.Sc.) program in Geoinformatics or a related field that involves:
Remote sensing
GIS
Spatial modeling
Spatial analysis
Digital image processing for GIS and similar subjects
note: The scholarship program applies to students enrolled in the second year of their postgraduate course.
benefits: A total of 10 selected students will receive an annual scholarship of ₹,100,000. Additionally, they will receive an ArcGIS Personal Use License valid for 1 year.
documents: A scanned copy of the student’s address proof
A scanned copy of the college ID-Card
First-year marksheet/certificates
Synopsis of projects done in the first year
A Statement of Purpose (SoP) in 250 words

name: INDIA FELLOW SOCIAL LEADERSHIP PROGRAM 2024-25
reward: A monthly stipend of up to 22,000 and other benefits
eligibility: Graduate students
about: India Fellow is an 18-month leadership program for curious professionals from various walks of life, willing to live and work alongside communities and find their leadership potential to bring social change. It is an experiential journey to immerse in our country's realities and be our best version to build a better future. Since 2010, we have had 250 fellows from varied academic and professional backgrounds who have worked across the country with 80+ partner organisations in innovative projects. They are supported by mentors who are leaders from various walks of life and a unique pedagogy. Fellows have been leading social ventures and solving complex problems across healthcare, livelihoods and value chain improvement, financial inclusion, access to education, social inclusion and equity, migration, climate change, human rights, governance, and more
Eligible: Applicant must be between 20 to 28 years of age
Is already a graduate (in any discipline) or will be by July 2016 before fellowship begins
Applicant must be an Indian citizen or a person of Indian origin
Applicant must be ready for a 13-month full-time commitment to the fellowship
Please ensure you have read, understood and align along the philosophy and values of this fellowship.
note: The fellow also gets a modest but adequate stipend from the host
benefits: An opportunity to receive a unique 13-month training program and attend
documents: No documents are required at the time of application.
How_to_apply: Step 1: Go to the website and read all the details before applying.
Step 2: Write down your personal information and click next, then write your educational information and click next to answer the descriptive questions:
Please tell us what are you pursuing currently. What are your motivations for doing the same (less than 250 words)
What do you think are your strengths that will help you excel in a challenging setup like the fellowship? (less than 250 words)
Why do you want to join India Fellow? How does this program meet your future goals? (less than 250 words)
What is the social change you want to bring about and why? How do you think you can contribute? (less than 500 words)
Step 3: Now upload your latest CV, PDF, Doc, or Docx files only.
Step 4: Now click to submit your application.

name: NEXTGEN EDU SCHOLARSHIP 2024-25
eligibility: Students studying in Class 11
about: As a part of Corporate Responsibility program, EY Global Delivery Services has partnered with Buddy4Study for NextGen Edu Scholarship 2024-25. The initiative aims to support academically promising and economically less privileged students in India. Specifically designed for Class 11 students enrolled in private or government schools across the country, the program provides financial assistance of INR 15,000 to enable them to pursue their education.EY Global Delivery Services (EY GDS), a division of Ernst & Young (EY), is an integrated service delivery center network that provides innovative, scalable, and customized business services to EY member firms. Present in ten countries (Argentina, China, Hungary, India, Mexico, Poland, the Philippines, Spain, and the United Kingdom) and 21 cities, EY GDS works with EY member firms across the globe to build a better working world by helping EY and its clients to be agile, nimble, and efficient.
Eligible: Students studying in Class 11 are eligible to apply.
Applicants must be enrolled in any private/government school across India.
Candidates must have secured a minimum of 60% in their Class 10.
They must have an annual family income of up to INR 3 lakh from all sources.
Children of employees of Buddy4Study and EY Global Delivery Services are not eligible to participate in the scholarship.
note: Preference will be given to students from the locations of Karnataka, Delhi National Capital Region, Maharashtra, Telangana, West Bengal, Kerala & Tamil Nadu.
This grant is open to all and encourages girls, single-parent children, orphans, transgender persons, and PwD students to come forward and apply.
benefits: Scholarship of INR 15,000
documents: Class 10 marksheet,
A government-issued address proof (Aadhaar card),
Proof of family income such as ITR, salary slips, income certificate from the concerned government authority, etc.,
Proof of admission (college/school ID card, academic fee receipt or fee structure),
Bank account details of the applicant or institute,
Transgender certificate issued by any NGO/GoI or competent authority (if applicable),
Recent photograph.
How_to_apply: Click on the ‘Apply Now’ button below,
Login to Buddy4Study with your registered ID and land on the ‘Application Form Page’.
If not registered, register at Buddy4Study with your email/mobile/Gmail account.
You will now be redirected to the ‘NextGen Edu Scholarship 2024-25’ application form page.
Click on the ‘Start Application’ button to begin the application process.
Fill in the required details in the online application form.
Upload relevant documents.
Accept the ‘Terms and Conditions’ and click on ‘Preview’.
If all the details filled in by the applicant are correctly showing on the preview screen, click on the ‘Submit’ button to complete the application process.

This scholarship aims to provide reimbursement of the tuition and other compulsory fees to SC, ST and OBC students studying in recognized public schools in Delhi. The selected students will receive a reimbursement of tuition, lab, and library fees up to ₹48,000 or the actual amount paid, whichever is less.
Eligible: Be a domicile in Delhi

Not have less than 70% attendance in the preceding year
Have an annual family income of not more than ₹3,00,000

name: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC Students Studying In Classes 9 and 10, Goa 2024-25
eligibility: SC Students of Class 9 and 10
about: Pre-Matric Scholarship for SC Students Studying In Classes 9 and 10, Goa 2024-25 is an initiative of the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Government of India to provide financial assistance to Scheduled Caste (SC) students who are studying in Class 9 and 10. The key objective of the scholarship is to minimise the incidence of dropout among the SC category of students in the transition from elementary to the secondary stage. The selected students will receive a consolidated academic allowance of up to ₹7,000 per annum and an extra allowance for disabled students.
Eligible: Be an Indian citizen
Be a domicile of Goa
Belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category
Be a full-time student in Class 9 or 10 from a recognised institution
Have a family income of less than ₹2,50,000 per annum
note: The disability is defined under the relevant Section of Rights of the Persons with Disability Act, 2016.
The appropriate Government shall designate persons, having requisite qualifications and experience, as certifying authorities, who shall be competent to issue the certificate of disability.
The award will be subject to satisfactory academic performance and 75% attendance every academic year.
benefits: The award will be subject to satisfactory academic performance and 75% attendance every academic year
documents: One passport-size photograph with the student's signature
Self-attested copies of certificates and mark sheets for previously passed exams
Certificate of caste signed by an authorised Revenue Officer, not below the rank of Tehsildar
The income certificate of the parents
Academic transcripts, including marksheets and certificates

be domiciled in Goa
belong to the Scheduled Caste (SC) category

If a student is awarded two scholarships simultaneously, they can choose one and formally communicate their preference to the awarding authorities through their institution's Head.

note: Applicants, who after completing one stage of education, continue studying in the same stage but in a different subject e.g. pursuing I.Sc. after an I.A., or opting for a B.Com. after B.A., or enrolling in an M.A. program in a different subject are ineligible.
Applicants who, have already completed their education in one professional line, for instance, pursuing L.L.B. after B.T./B.Ed are ineligible.
benefits: Tuition fees shall be paid merely to online/correspondence students.
documents: Aadhaar card of the applicant

Original fee receipts for the current academic session
Fee structure with the official letterhead of the concerned institute with stamp and signature of the head of the institute

Certificate from the institution confirming that the student is not receiving any other scholarship or stipend

about: Post Matric Scholarship to SBC Students, Maharashtra 2024-25 is an initiative of the VJNT, OBC, and SBC Welfare Department, Government of Maharashtra for the Special Backward Classes (SBC) category students pursuing post-matric studies. Maintenance allowance and exam fees are disbursed to the applicant upon promotion to the next higher class.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Persons with Disability, Maharashtra 2024-25
reward: A monthly maintenance allowance up to 21200 and other benefits
eligibility: Disabled students studying in Class 11 to Ph.D. level
about: Post-Matric Scholarship for Persons with Disability, Maharashtra 2023-24 is an initiative of the Department of Social Justice and Special Assistance, Government of Maharashtra. It provides financial assistance to disabled students enrolled in Class 11 to Ph.D. Selected scholars will receive a monthly maintenance allowance of up to ₹1,200 and other benefits.
Eligible: be a domicile of Maharashtra
have a disability of 40% or more, as certified by a competent authority
be studying in Class 11 to Ph.D. level at a government or government-aided institution in Maharashtra
have been admitted through the CAP process
note: The scholarship will not be applicable if the candidate fails or quits the same course/incomplete course.

The scholarship will not be applicable for a second time if the candidate applies based on HSC/SSC/Degree criteria. (Note:- Applications should be progressive; only one course is allowed per application.)
Students studying in a recognised institute or college outside Maharashtra are eligible, but they must be residents of Maharashtra.
Students pursuing post-graduation in the medical field who are not allowed to practice outside the institute e.g., interns or residents receiving a stipend are eligible.

If a student in arts, science, or commerce discontinues their course and applies for a professional course, a technical education certificate/diploma/degree is applicable for the scholarship. However, if the candidate fails in Group A, the scholarship will not be applicable.
For Groups B, C, D, and E: If the student fails, they will not be eligible for this scheme.
For Group A: If the student fails once, they may be eligible for this scheme, but they will not be eligible if they fail a second time during their entire education.
Students enrolled in distance education from a recognised university/institution where non-refundable fees must be paid will receive an annual grant of ₹500 for book and material purchases.
Candidates can only apply for the Shahu Maharaj Merit Scholarship with this scheme. They cannot apply for another scheme concurrently.
If a candidate is working full-time, they are not eligible.

If a candidate is a government hosteller and does not receive books and stationery from the hostel, they are eligible for an additional 1/3 of the maintenance allowance allocated for hostel residents.
Students residing in a college/institute hostel or any other recognised hostel who pay hostel fees will receive the maintenance allowance at the hosteller rate.

about: NSFDC Education Loan Scheme for SC Students is an initiative of the National Scheduled Castes Finance and Development Corporation (NSFDC) Scheme for Scheduled Caste (SC) students pursuing professional or technical courses. It provides loans to cover tuition, hostel, books, and other educational expenses. Under this scheme, cover tuition, hostel, books, and other educational expenses will be covered. Eligible students can borrow up to ₹30,00,000 for domestic studies and ₹40,00,000 for overseas studies. The scheme offers lower interest rates compared to commercial banks and flexible repayment options. Loans will be disbursed through State Channelizing Agencies (SCAs), and students must meet specific income and caste criteria to qualify.NSFDC Education Loan Scheme for SC Students

note: A rebate of 0.5% will be provided to female applicants
